text:
There once was a young boy named Lucas who loved collecting sea shells on lonely beaches at the end of summer. He would collect enough shells each summer to fill a small jar. Every autumn he'd visit the same beaches to find the new seashells. One day, while searching for his favorite shell—a delicate lavender bubblegum pink—

Oops, I love animals!

It turned out there was a young sea lion who preferred purple velvet pillows. Every autumn, the cheerful sea lion visited the same pier, searching for just the right hue to stuff into its pile of plushies.

Lucas noticed the sea lion and its plushie pillow pile. Every year he would admire these cheerful creatures through binoculars but never try to approach them. One day, while picking his favorite shell, he realized he should take a closer look.

Gently reaching out, Lucas gently knocked on the sea lion's oceanic ear. To his awe-struck delight, the sea lion lifted its head slowly, blinked twice
